response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering capabilities with 20+ years of experience and excellent practices in testing and infrastructure, they lack nearly all domain-specific requirements for this embedded networking security role. Critically missing: native-level Japanese proficiency (required), deep C expertise for UNIX embedded systems, Linux kernel investigation experience, and security domain knowledge (encryption, authentication protocols, network security technologies). This represents a significant career pivot requiring 1-2 years of specialized learning in a security-critical domain.
strengths: 20+ years of versatile software development experience across multiple domains and programming languages demonstrating strong foundational engineering skills
Proven expertise in testing practices, CI/CD infrastructure, Docker, and full-stack development across diverse tech stacks
Some low-level systems work experience with C/C++ at National Instruments and familiarity with Linux environments
concerns: No demonstrated proficiency in security technologies (encryption, key exchange, user-authentication systems, attack detection, or network security protocols)
Missing native-level Japanese language proficiency—a stated requirement—and no embedded systems or LAN-switch networking experience documented in resume
No Linux kernel investigation, modification experience, or knowledge of required networking protocols (Layer 2/3, MACsec, SNMP, NETCONF, IEEE 802.1X)

key_factors.factor: Security domain expertise and embedded firmware knowledge gap
Linux kernel and networking protocols experience absent
